executiveSummary.text: US stocks closed slightly lower Monday as Iran re-closed Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, reversing Friday's record-high surge. S&P 500 -0.22%, Nasdaq -0.25%, Dow flat.
Oil prices spiked 6-7% with Brent at $95.48 and WTI at $89.61 as Hormuz closure and tanker boarding reignited supply fears. Energy sector outperformed.
Kevin Warsh's Fed Chair confirmation hearing today at 10am — markets watching for rate path signals. Prediction markets see no rate change after July 2026 at 78.5%.
Gold holds near record at $4,833/oz, silver at $80.87 — geopolitical risk and dollar weakness sustaining safe-haven bid. Copper at $6.03/lb, up 11% monthly.
Lam Research (LRCX) reports Q3 earnings tomorrow April 22 — key for semicap cycle validation. ASML and TSMC already posted strong results but stocks sold off.

usGlobal.what: Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz; oil spikes 6-7%
usGlobal.why: Weekend escalation reverses Friday's optimism. 20% of global oil transit threatened. Energy stocks rallied; transport and consumer discretionary lagged.
usGlobal.implication: Short-term inflation risk from energy costs; supports oil/energy positions but weighs on rate-cut hopes. Energy portion of your scarcity thesis gets validated.

usGlobal.what: ASML raised 2026 guidance to €36-40B after Q1 beat (€8.8B rev, €2.8B net income); stock still sold off post-earnings
usGlobal.why: Classic buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news. Strong AI-driven demand confirmed but valuation already rich. TSMC also posted strong results with stock dip.
usGlobal.implication: AI capex cycle intact — your scarcity thesis on semicap equipment remains validated. Post-earnings dips may be accumulation opportunities.

usGlobal.what: Lam Research reports Q3 FY26 earnings tomorrow April 22 — consensus EPS $1.35, rev $5.70B
usGlobal.why: LRCX is a key portfolio holding and critical for semicap cycle validation. Q2 beat by 8.7% on EPS. BNP Paribas warns semicap stocks may peak late 2026.
usGlobal.implication: Watch LRCX guidance closely — if beat/raise, confirms your equipment scarcity thesis. BNP peak warning worth noting for position sizing.

usGlobal.what: NVIDIA next earnings May 20 — consensus EPS $1.77; stock flat YTD after April rally from March lows
usGlobal.why: NVDA recovered in April but still underperforming broader market. May 20 is the next catalyst. Options typically see 8-10% swing post-earnings.
usGlobal.implication: Core position — no action needed until earnings approach. Current flat YTD could mean upside if Q1 data center revenue surprises again.

usGlobal.what: Warsh Fed Chair confirmation hearing today at 10am ET
usGlobal.why: Warsh is Trump's pick to replace Powell. Key question: will he signal hawkish or dovish tilt? Prediction markets price no rate change after July 2026.
usGlobal.implication: If Warsh signals hawkish stance, growth/tech could pull back. If dovish, risk-on could resume. Your barbell structure hedges either way.

singapore.what: STI gained 0.5% to 5,008 — regional rebound led by MLT; Singapore banks resilient
singapore.why: Safe-haven flows into Singapore amid Middle East uncertainty. OCBC hit all-time high in April with 4.3% forward yield.
singapore.implication: SG dividend portfolio benefiting from risk-off rotation. OCBC outperforming DBS and UOB in 2026 — JP Morgan downgrade of OCBC to underweight is contrarian signal.

singapore.what: CapitaLand Ascendas REIT receives 170% excess applications for preferential offering at S$2.35/unit
singapore.why: Strong demand signals investor confidence in industrial REIT recovery. CICT also acquiring retail complex for S$3B.
singapore.implication: REIT recovery thesis gaining traction. Your Ascendas, CICT, MLT, FCT positions well-positioned for rate stabilization tailwind.

singapore.what: PhillipCapital replaces Singtel with Sembcorp in Absolute 10 portfolio
singapore.why: Sembcorp's renewables pivot and Alinta Energy acquisition ($2.1B loan) driving earnings upgrade. Singtel's 45% 2025 rally may have run ahead.
singapore.implication: Both holdings have different catalysts — Sembcorp on energy transition, Singtel on value unlock from associates. Hold both.

commodities.what: Gold holds near record at $4,833/oz; WGC warns equity rally on 'fragile foundations'
commodities.why: Central bank buying (China), weaker dollar, and Hormuz risk all supporting gold. Silver at $80.87.
commodities.implication: Your gold position is a core hedge performing as designed. No need to add — let it ride as geopolitical risk hedge.

commodities.what: Copper at $6.03/lb, up 11% monthly — AI data center and energy transition demand accelerating
commodities.why: Supply deficit structural. Copper up 27% YoY. Tariff-related front-running also contributing.
commodities.implication: Copper scarcity thesis directly aligned with your AI infra + metals positions. Structural demand from data centers and grid buildout.

commodities.what: Uranium at $86.65/lb, up 33% YoY — nuclear renaissance theme intact
commodities.why: AI energy demand driving nuclear expansion globally. Cameco highlighted as top 2026 energy pick.
commodities.implication: Uranium position benefits from long-term AI energy demand thesis. Short-term range-bound but structural uptrend intact.

narratives.title: Hormuz Whiplash: Ceasefire Chaos
narratives.description: Iran opened Hormuz Friday (SPX record highs), then re-closed it over the weekend (oil +7%). Ceasefire talks with Pakistan as mediator. Market pricing gradual de-escalation but headline risk remains extreme.
narratives.priceImpact: high — oil ±5-10% per headline; equity sector rotation

narratives.title: AI Capex Cycle Confirmed but Semis Sell the News
narratives.description: ASML and TSMC both beat and raised guidance, confirming AI spending boom intact. But both stocks dipped post-earnings — valuation exhaustion. LRCX tomorrow is the next test.
narratives.priceImpact: medium — sector rotation within tech, not a trend reversal

narratives.title: Fed Regime Change: Warsh Replacing Powell
narratives.description: Kevin Warsh faces Senate Banking Committee today. Key risk: hawkish pivot could derail rate-cut expectations. Markets currently pricing only 1 cut in 2026 vs. 2 priced earlier.
narratives.priceImpact: medium-to-high — rate path expectations could shift materially

narratives.title: Commodity Super Cycle Accelerating
narratives.description: Gold at $4,833, copper at $6.03, uranium at $86.65 — all structurally rising on AI energy demand, de-dollarization, and supply deficits. WGC warns equity rally is on fragile foundations.
narratives.priceImpact: sustained — gradual repricing higher, not a spike

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil at $95+ flows through to Singapore electricity tariffs and transport costs, pressuring REIT tenant margins and consumer spending — but boosts Sembcorp's conventional energy revenue.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Net neutral for your SG portfolio — Sembcorp benefits, REITs face minor headwind.

secondOrderEffects.text: Hormuz disruptions could accelerate China's strategic stockpiling of commodities, driving further gold/copper/uranium demand — reinforcing your scarcity thesis.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Positive for metals and uranium positions. Geopolitical de-risking = commodity upside.

secondOrderEffects.text: If Warsh signals hawkish tilt, SGD may strengthen further vs USD on safe-haven flows — dampening USD-denominated returns when converted back to SGD.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Monitor SGD/USD — currently 1.269. A move to 1.25 would reduce USD portfolio returns by ~1.5% in SGD terms.

investment.thesis: Add to copper/scarcity metals on Hormuz-driven dip
investment.trigger: Copper pulling back below $6.00/lb on any de-escalation headline
investment.whyNow: Structural AI energy demand + supply deficit + de-dollarization = multi-year tailwind. Temporary dip from geopolitical relief is a buying opportunity.
investment.invalidator: Global recession cutting industrial demand sharply

investment.thesis: Accumulate SG REITs on rate stabilization
investment.trigger: 10Y yield staying below 4.3% and Fed cutting at least once in 2026
investment.whyNow: REITs at inflection point — Ascendas 170% oversubscription shows institutional demand returning. Rate stabilization is the key catalyst.
investment.invalidator: Rates rising above 4.5% or Singapore property cooling measures

trading.thesis: LRCX earnings straddle — volatility play ahead of April 22 report
trading.trigger: LRCX options pricing ~5% move; buy straddle if implied vol is reasonable
trading.whyNow: Semicap cycle validation. ASML/TSMC sold off despite beats — LRCX could swing either way. Straddle captures move regardless of direction.
trading.invalidator: Implied volatility too expensive (check IV vs historical)

trading.thesis: Short oil rally on any Hormuz reopening headline
trading.trigger: Brent above $95 with news of Iran re-opening Hormuz or peace talks progress
trading.whyNow: Oil spike is headline-driven, not fundamental. $95+ Brent overstates sustained supply disruption risk. Mean reversion likely on de-escalation.
trading.invalidator: Full-scale Hormuz blockade confirmed with military escalation

watchlist.thesis: Warsh confirmation hearing — Fed rate path implications
watchlist.trigger: Watch for hawkish vs dovish language at 10am ET hearing
watchlist.whyNow: If Warsh signals fewer/no rate cuts, growth stocks and REITs could pull back — creating better entry points.
watchlist.invalidator: Warsh confirms independence and data-dependent approach (neutral/bullish)

watchlist.thesis: Tesla earnings Wednesday — Magnificent Seven sentiment barometer
watchlist.trigger: TSLA reports after market Wednesday — first Mag 7 report of the season
watchlist.whyNow: Tesla results set tone for tech earnings. Weak demand/guidance could pressure NVDA, AVGO, MSFT ahead of their reports.
watchlist.invalidator: Tesla beats on margins and delivery guidance — bullish for tech sentiment

riskWatch.name: Hormuz Escalation
riskWatch.description: Strait of Hormuz re-closed; Iran considering peace talks but timeline uncertain. Oil could spike to $110+ if blockade extends. S&P 500 could pull back 3-5% on sustained closure.
riskWatch.mitigation: Gold position as hedge. Energy exposure via Sembcorp. Barbell structure provides downside protection.

riskWatch.name: Fed Hawkish Pivot
riskWatch.description: Warsh confirmation hearing today. If he signals fewer rate cuts or hawkish inflation stance, growth valuations could compress. 10Y yield could push back above 4.5%.
riskWatch.mitigation: SG dividend portfolio provides income regardless of rate direction. Cash position (26%) provides dry powder for dips.

riskWatch.name: Semicap Cycle Peak
riskWatch.description: BNP Paribas warns semicap equipment stocks may peak in late 2026/early 2027 based on historical cycle patterns. ASML and TSMC sold off despite strong earnings.
riskWatch.mitigation: Trim into strength on LRCX, AMAT, KLAC if they beat earnings. Don't add to semicap positions at current levels — wait for pullbacks.

riskWatch.name: SGD Strength Eroding USD Returns
riskWatch.description: SGD strengthening to 1.269 vs USD on safe-haven flows. If trend continues, USD-denominated portfolio returns diluted when measured in SGD.
riskWatch.mitigation: Natural hedge via SGD-denominated holdings (banks, REITs, CPF). Monitor but don't over-hedge.

keyEvents.text: Warsh Fed Chair confirmation hearing — 10am ET today
US March Retail Sales data — 8:30am ET today (consensus +1.3% MoM)
LRCX Q3 FY26 earnings — April 22 after close (EPS est. $1.35, rev $5.70B)
Tesla Q1 earnings — April 23 after close (first Mag 7 report)
Iran peace talks consideration — Pakistan mediation, timing unclear

bottomLine.whatMatters: Hormuz whiplash is the dominant risk — oil spikes and equity wobbles will persist until there's a durable ceasefire. Warsh hearing today could reshape rate expectations. Semicap earnings (LRCX tomorrow) validate or challenge the AI capex cycle thesis.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold positions. Don't chase oil spike or panic-sell on Hormuz headlines. Watch Warsh hearing for rate signals. If LRCX beats, resist adding — wait for post-earnings dip. If copper dips below $6.00 on de-escalation, consider adding to metals position.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Day-to-day Hormuz headlines (noise until there's a structural resolution). ASML/TSMC post-earnings dips (sector rotation, not cycle reversal). OCBC downgrade by JP Morgan (contrarian signal, not actionable).
